肉眼看到的 # Context History 也没有减小，一直增加，也是正常的？

decision.archive_summary: The conversation tested VS Code Context Minor GC in a new Codex thread under the constraint 'only do perceptual/manual tests, no code changes.' Two short cases were run and judged successful: returning to the travel topic correctly yielded Kyoto, and returning to the editor topic correctly yielded Zed; in both cases GC applied and promptReductionRatio was high (~0.76-0.79). The user then questioned why visible Context(估算) and # Context History did not shrink. The resolved explanation is that this is usually normal: those visible counters are a different measurement surface and can continue increasing even when GC successfully reduces the next-turn raw working set.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest question was answered directly and the thread mostly closes on an interpretation of observability metrics, so resolve fits better than continue. The early step-by-step test-driving turns are solved and are good eviction candidates. What should survive is the semantic result of the test setup and conclusions: this session is about validating Context Minor GC behavior, both manual cases passed, and visible Context(估算)/# Context History not decreasing does not by itself mean GC failed.

applied.archiveSummary: The conversation tested VS Code Context Minor GC in a new Codex thread under the constraint 'only do perceptual/manual tests, no code changes.' Two short cases were run and judged successful: returning to the travel topic correctly yielded Kyoto, and returning to the editor topic correctly yielded Zed; in both cases GC applied and promptReductionRatio was high (~0.76-0.79). The user then questioned why visible Context(估算) and # Context History did not shrink. The resolved explanation is that this is usually normal: those visible counters are a different measurement surface and can continue increasing even when GC successfully reduces the next-turn raw working set.
